title: Export complete app as a single self-contained HTML file

requirements.text: Create a standalone, self-contained single HTML file (frontend/public/standalone.html) that duplicates the full current application. The file must inline all CSS (Tailwind via CDN or inlined styles), all JavaScript logic (React via CDN UMD builds), all UI content, translations, and mock/static data so it runs without any backend canister or build toolchain. All fonts, icons, and assets must be loaded via CDN links or inlined as base64 data URIs. The file should be fully functional as a static preview/demo with hardcoded sample data replacing backend calls.

quotes: make the complete code to one html file as a duplicate

acceptanceCriteria: A single file frontend/public/standalone.html exists and opens correctly in a browser without a local server or build step.

All pages and navigation (Home, About, Share Experience, Dashboard, Community Chat, Available Students, Wishlist, Post Detail) are represented and navigable within the single file using hash-based or in-page routing.
The file includes all CSS styles either via Tailwind CDN or inlined, matching the existing violet/neutral color palette and theme.
Sample/mock data is hardcoded to populate posts, college connect tips, feedback, community chat messages, and student profiles so every section appears populated.
Year selection modal appears on first load and year badges display correctly for all four years (1st–4th).
No external canister calls are made; all data interactions use in-memory JavaScript state.
The file renders correctly in modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) without console errors.
Open Graph meta tags and the page title 'Mem Nerchukunnavi | మేము నేర్చుకున్నవి' are present in the <head>.

constraints: The output must be a single .html file — no separate .js, .css, or asset files.
Do not modify any existing frontend source files or the backend.
All immutable frontend paths must remain untouched.
nonGoals: Replacing or modifying the existing React/Motoko application.
Connecting the standalone HTML file to any live backend canister.
Supporting Internet Identity authentication in the standalone file.
Real-time data updates or persistence in the standalone file.
